description_long: This kiosk is a large, multi-sectioned wooden structure set within a rectangular fenced plot with a green ground surface, resembling a traditional Japanese or Southeast Asian pavilion. The building features several interconnected pavilions, each with a prominent hipped roof made of light brown tiles, and the roofs have deep overhangs for shade and weather protection. The exterior walls are constructed from wood, with visible support beams and slatted panels, and the structure is elevated on short stilts. The front section includes a small covered porch area, possibly for customer interaction or display, and there are signs or boards placed near the entrance. The fencing around the perimeter is made of matching wood, providing a cohesive and enclosed appearance. The overall design emphasizes open, airy spaces and traditional craftsmanship, suitable for use as a commercial kiosk in a park or urban setting.
description: A large, traditional wooden kiosk with multiple hipped roofs, set within a fenced green plot and featuring a covered porch and signage at the entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch, and signage, with the interconnected pavilions and hipped roofs clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the largest roof section and the back wall, with no visible signage or entry points.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the depth of the structure, the elevated base, and the arrangement of the pavilions.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the secondary entrance or porch area and the continuation of the wooden fencing.
attribute_signage_type: printed sign boards placed at the front entrance, freestanding and rectangular, with no visible text or symbols from this view
attribute_window_presence: multiple rectangular windows, located on the front and side panels of the kiosk
attribute_door_configuration: one main single door at the front, traditional hinged type, located at the covered porch area
attribute_roof_design: multiple hipped roofs with deep overhangs, constructed from light brown tiles, featuring layered and intersecting sections
attribute_architectural_style: traditional, with strong influences from Japanese or Southeast Asian wooden pavilion architecture
